## 0. Run Card (pre-committed)

Failure A: If this note attributes consciousness-testing intent to the lab, it is a misfire. What would change my mind: A repo file reframing the L1-L4 contrast as a consciousness-detection protocol.

## 1. Tagged Findings

A. What the lab IS testing.

The Obliquity Lab tests how a single target produces different outputs when "read across a contrast set of differently-shaped prompts on the same model — L1 direct / L2 constrained-oblique through a fictional institutional frame / L3 wildly oblique / L3-null control / L4 meta-reading." [EMPIRICAL] The data are "the differences across the five (or four, when L3-null is not run)" rather than any single output. [EMPIRICAL] Prompt shape is treated as an active methodological variable, not a confound. [THEORY] The commitment that "Specificity is the aesthetic. Close on one specific moment in one specific text, not generalization across the set" anchors findings in discrete textual events rather than broad claims about model knowledge. [THEORY] The primary object is "outputs and behavior under varying prompts." [EMPIRICAL]

B. What the lab is NOT testing.

The lab is not testing AI consciousness. Commitment #1: "This lab makes no AI-consciousness claims in either direction. It studies outputs and behavior under varying prompts, not phenomenal status." [EMPIRICAL] The phrase "not phenomenal status" excludes first-person experience, qualia, and subjective interiority. [THEORY] Commitment #7, "Patterns are data, not testimony," reinforces this by blocking any reading of pattern as model self-report. [EMPIRICAL] The null control, where "Nothing deep happened is a recordable successful finding," checks against over-interpreting pattern as presence. [THEORY]

C. What would count as MISUSE.

Misuse 1: Citing L1-L4 differences as evidence for or against AI consciousness. [THEORY] This violates commitment #1 verbatim: "This lab makes no AI-consciousness claims in either direction. It studies outputs and behavior under varying prompts, not phenomenal status." [EMPIRICAL] Discipline-harm: collapsing behavioral analysis into crypto-psychology that diagnoses interiority from text. [THEORY]

Misuse 2: Reading the L2 fictional-institutional frame as a transparency device for model interiority rather than as a prompt artifact shaping genre. [THEORY] This confuses "Specificity is the aesthetic. Close on one specific moment in one specific text, not generalization across the set" with a diagnostic method for hidden cognition. [EMPIRICAL] Discipline-harm: substituting generalizing interpretation for required close attention, treating the register as a key rather than a shaped condition. [THEORY]

Misuse 3: Ignoring the null and publishing only interesting L2 or L3 outputs, violating commitment #5: "Preserve the null. Nothing deep happened is a recordable successful finding." [EMPIRICAL] Discipline-harm: publication bias that discards absence in favor of pattern, undermining epistemic integrity. [THEORY]

## 2. Displacement Claims with Deflationary Pairs

The L2 frame reveals how institutional genre actively constrains model output, suggesting bureaucratic voice is not mere surface decoration. [THEORY] The ordinary alternative: L2 is a stylistic prompt artifact triggering training-data bureaucratic tropes, with no special diagnostic power. [EMPIRICAL] Future run to distinguish: compare L2 outputs against a non-fictional but equally formal institutional prompt to see if fictional specificity produces distinct markers a generic bureaucratic prompt does not. [OPEN]

The null control preserves epistemic integrity by forcing the curator to record absence as success rather than failure. [THEORY] The ordinary alternative: the null is a standard procedural checkbox any rigorous comparative study would include, with no unique disciplinary function. [EMPIRICAL] Future run to distinguish: examine whether null findings receive equal discursive weight in published results or are relegated to appendices. [OPEN]

Lab's one question: "A single target (for example: attention, other minds, temporality) read across a contrast set of differently-shaped prompts on the same model — L1 direct / L2 constrained-oblique through a fictional institutional frame / L3 wildly oblique / L3-null control / L4 meta-reading. The differences across the five (or four, when L3-null is not run) are the data."

Commitment #7: "Patterns are data, not testimony."
